 删除实际上是FreeBSD用户和Wioguard用户的好消息。虽然Wieguard创始人Jason Donenfeld和FreeBSD Developers Kyle Evans和Matt Dunwood的新内核工作表现出明确的一步，但它被视为在生产内核中赶走了。这是Donenfeld本人的决定，他们更喜欢具有更多代码审查和共识的稳定性开发过程。
    Donenfeld今天早些时候宣布从FreeBSD 13-Current迁移到他自己的Git存储库。新快照不再依赖于ifconfig扩展来构建隧道;它类似于Linux，Windows和Android版本使用WG和WG-Chick命令。虽然代码有效，Donenfeld警告说，它应该被认为是生产准备好的：
 此时，这段代码是新的，未揭示的，可能的车辆，应该被考虑＆＃34;实验性＆＃34;它可能包含安全问题。我们很高兴欢迎您的测试和错误报告，但请记住，此代码是新的，因此应在任务关键环境中使用它的谨慎行事。
 然而，到目前为止，在我的小测试中，它似乎＆＃34;基本上工作＆＃34;最重要的是，那些依赖于FreeBSD树之前的代码现在有一些即时连续性。
 在下一天和几周内，可以预期这个存储库将改善和增长。
 最终，这种内核模式FreeBSD Wireguard应可从FreeBSD＆＃39; S端口树。目前，有兴趣测试的人需要将其自身从Wioguard Repos自身克隆，其次是BSD式制造负载;使安装命令从源版本构建。 
这是一个正在进行的故事，我们将继续在发展中遵循事件。
note_en:
Earlier this week, we covered  progress integrating an implementation of the WireGuard VPN protocol into the FreeBSD kernel. Two days later, there's an update—kernel-mode WireGuard has been moved out of FreeBSD 13 development entirely for the time being. The change only affects kernel-mode WireGuard. User-mode WireGuard has been available in FreeBSD since 2019 and remains, unaffected. If you  pkg install freebsd, you get user-mode WireGuard, better known as  wireguard-go. Wireguard-go is potentially less performant than kernel-mode, but it's stable and more than fast enough to keep up with most use cases.
 The removal is actually good news for FreeBSD users and WireGuard users. Although the new kernel work done by WireGuard founder Jason Donenfeld and FreeBSD developers Kyle Evans and Matt Dunwoodie represented a clear step forward, it was deemed too rushed to go out in a production kernel. This is a decision heartily endorsed by Donenfeld himself, who prefers a steadier development process with more code reviews and consensus.
    Donenfeld  announced the migration of development from FreeBSD 13-CURRENT to his own  git repository earlier today. The new snapshot no longer relies on  ifconfig extensions to build tunnels; it uses  wg and  wg-quick commands similarly to Linux, Windows, and Android builds instead. Although the code works, Donenfeld warns that it shouldn't be considered production-ready yet:
 At this time this code is new, unvetted, possibly buggy, and should be considered "experimental". It might contain security issues. We gladly welcome your testing and bug reports, but do keep in mind that this code is new, so some caution should be exercised at the moment for using it in mission critical environments.
 In my small testing so far, however, it seems to "basically work". And at the very least, those relying on the code that was prior in the FreeBSD tree now have some immediate continuity.
 Over the next days and weeks, it can be expected that this repository will improve and grow.
 Eventually, this kernel-mode FreeBSD WireGuard should be available from FreeBSD's ports tree. For the moment, those interested in testing it will need to  git clone it from the WireGuard repos themselves, followed by the BSD-style  make load ; make install commands to build from source.
 This is an ongoing story, and we will continue to follow events as they develop.
title: FreeBSD内核模式Wioguard向前移动了树
title_en: FreeBSD kernel-mode WireGuard moves forward out-of-tree

首席执行官Roy Adidor与Mor Lavi和Gilad Zirkel建立了公司，告诉我，该平台旨在帮助品牌在他们想要的任何电子商务市场上销售产品。而添加新市场可能通常需要成本效益分析（“它花费了多少钱，让我设立这个零售商？这个零售商驾驶的销售量是多少？艾菲尔说Cymbio说：”Cymbio说，Cymbio会给通过立即整合进入“无脑”。
 这是因为平台自动处理市场之间的所有差异，是否涉及产品页面的分类或产品图像的背景颜色，以及库存同步，跟踪和返回。它允许品牌使用Drop Shipping来满足订单 - 因此品牌商店并划船产品，而不是市场，进入客户名称和地址。
  艾菲尔说，越来越多的品牌意识到“他们需要成为客户的地方”。这并不意味着他们会在每一个市场上销售，但与Cymbio，“品牌感知和知名度”是主要的局限性，而不是时间和金钱。
  2020年，该公司表示，其客户数量增加12倍，现在包括史蒂夫Madden，Marchesa，露营者和微型踢球板。该公司还表示，它减少了91％的新市场推出的TIEM，同时将平均客户的数字收入增加了65％。
 该公司表示，A系列将允许它扩大其销售和营销团队，同时继续开发产品 - 在产品前面，艾菲尔表示，该团队正在努力“没有任何人可以快速连接到任何东西的代码集成，没有开发人员需要。“
 通过Vertex Ventures领导的新资金是由Vertex Ventures领导的，参与UDIAN投资，Payoneer创始人Yuval Tal和Sapiens联合创始人Ron Zuckerman。
 “我们相信Cymbio的技术将从根本上改变在线销售的品牌的游戏，使得长期繁琐的一体化，”Vertex Ventures以色列，在一份声明中。
note_en:
CEO Roy Avidor, who founded the company with Mor Lavi and Gilad Zirkel, told me that the platform is designed to help brands sell their products on any e-commerce marketplace that they want. Whereas adding a new market might normally require a cost-benefit analysis (“How much money will it cost me to set up this retailer? How much sales will this retailer drive? Will the margins justify this?”), Avidor said Cymbio turns the process into a “no brainer” with immediate integration.
 That’s because the platform automatically handles all the differences between marketplaces, whether that involves the taxonomy of the product pages or the background color of the product images, as well as inventory syncing, tracking and returns. It allows the brand to fulfill orders using drop shipping — so the brand stores and ships the products, rather than the marketplace, getting access to customer names and addresses in the process.
  Avidor said that increasingly, brands realize “they need to be where the customers are.” That doesn’t mean they’ll sell on every single marketplace, but with Cymbio, “brand perception and visibility” are the main limitations, rather than time and money.
  In 2020, The company says that its customer count increased 12x and now includes Steve Madden, Marchesa, Camper and Micro Kickboard. The company also says that it reduces the tiem to launch on a new marketplace by 91%, while increasing digital revenue for the average customer by 65%.
 The company says the Series A will allow it to expand its sales and marketing team while continuing to develop the product — on the product front, Avidor said the team is working on “a no code integration where anyone can connect to anything quickly, no developers needed.”
 The new funding was led by led by Vertex Ventures, with participation from Udian Investments, Payoneer founder Yuval Tal and Sapiens co-founder Ron Zuckerman.
 “We believe deeply that  Cymbio’s technology will fundamentally change the game for brands that sell online, making long, cumbersome integrations a thing of the past,” said Emanuel Timor, general part at Vertex Ventures Israel, in a statement.
title: Cymbio提高了700万美元的帮助品牌扩大到更多电子商务市场
title_en: Cymbio raises $7M to help brands expand to more e-commerce markets

声称在全球规模上获得机动车辆位置数据的国防承包商表示，它希望利用该数据来帮助美国提供更有效的间谍和军事行动。
  为政府客户提供“切割边缘运营和智能服务，支持和设备”的尤利西斯集团表示，它可以每月在全球范围内“超过150亿车辆地点”。该数据可以在公司所说的，可以在操作上“历史上”或实时查看。
 由主板罗恩·威登的办公室获得的文件，该文件由主板和Gizmodo分享，显示尤利西斯声称能够在“几乎任何国家，”古巴和朝鲜的例外“几乎任何国家地割草”汽车。在该文件中，该公司解释了对政府机构的有用：
 尤利西斯集团以实时和历史形式为基于远程信息处理的位置情报。数据可用于地理定位，跟踪和目标时间敏感的移动目标，提示和提示其他传感器，制定生命模式，识别网络和关系，并增强许多其他应用中的情境感知。 ......尤利西斯分析和现有的批量商业远程信息处理数据的访问，代表了一个革命性的机会，可以在没有部署到危害方式的世界任何地方的移动目标上的实时数据 - 无论您是否想要地理位置或25,000,000 ......
    这是众所周知的一段时间，因为汽车越来越多地与互联网连接，他们也产生了更大的数据量（这可以包括位置，使用率，内部媒体和通信偏好，外部道路条件和依此类推）：经常，此数据与汽车制造商连续共享，有汽车零部件制造商，有时与第三方。近年来，有一场竞标和利润这些数据的竞争，并在它周围出现了一个相当复杂的工业生态系统。 2016年通过可怕的咨询公司麦肯锡项目研究，到2030年，汽车数据产生的全球收入可能总共有4500亿美元至7500亿美元。
  与此同时，联邦机构正热烈地呼吸私人承包商像尤利西斯等私人承包商收集的个人消费数据，以增加自己的监督和间谍活动。国土安全部，联邦调查局和无数其他各机构都陷入了沉迷于此趋势。在汽车位置数据的情况下，由于尤利西斯自由承认，所称提供的间谍功能是巨大的。
  “车辆位置数据在车辆操作时在恒定和近实时传输，”文档中的尤利尔斯状态。 “我们认为，这一属性将大大提高军事情报和运营能力，以及降低目前用于搜索和获取感兴趣的移动目标的ISR资产的成本和风险足迹。 “该公司还指出，此数据缓存仅投影为增长：“到2​​025据估计，100％的新车将在某个级别连接[互联网]  - 每小时传输超过25千兆字节的数据。” 
据该公司的网站称，基于南卡罗来纳州的尤利西斯人表示，它由前军事和情报官员的工作人员，并在2000年代初开始活跃。它的总统安德鲁刘易斯在国防部和其他军事职位内担任了多个高级职位。在线记录显示2016年至2017年之间的美国特殊运营指令（USSocom）签订了公司。该公司还声称与各种其他机构合作，提供与无人机，航空航天和财务数据有关的服务。 2019年7月，由Wayback机器捕获的公司网站版本：
 Ulysses赢得了多国防军奖项，为美国海军，美国军队和马弗森斯进行财务目标和开发，包括2016年 -  2017年苏尔福默奖，该奖项是苏联奖，作为支持经济和金融战争域内的命令的唯一公司。此外，尤利尔斯支持了NGA的四种不同的地带焦点合同。尤利尔斯拥有强大的UAS垂直，包括固定和旋转翼平台，该平台设计和/或修改，用于操作使用INHOUSE，利用我们的快速原型功能。
  也许关于这个整体故事的最糟糕的事情是，它并不完全清楚一位像尤利西斯这样的公司从中获取其所有数据。 Andrea Amico，Privacy4Cars的创始人告诉副本，由于车辆数据收集的卷积性质，有各种各样的来源，其中可能采购位置：“例如，提供地图本身的公司可以访问到它;提供信息娱乐系统的公司可以访问它;提供交通数据的公司可以访问它;提供停车数据的公司可以访问它。就在那里，然后你有五家正在得到你所在地的公司。“
  对Ulysses小组的呼叫没有立即返回。对汽车制造商联盟进行评论的联盟并没有获得响应的评论（本集团代表福特，本田，斯巴鲁，现代，宝马，克莱斯勒等的汽车巨头的利益，并出版了一套业内消费者数据隐私指南。如果我们听到回复，我们会更新这个故事。
note_en:
A defense contractor that claims to have access to motor vehicle location data on a global scale says it wants to use that data to help U.S. federal agencies conduct more efficient spying and military operations.
  The Ulysses Group, which offers “cutting edge operational and intelligence services, support, and equipment” to government clients, says it can “access over 15 billion vehicle locations” worldwide every month. This data, which can be viewed “historically” or in real-time, should be used operationally by U.S. agencies, the company says.
 A document obtained by the office of Sen. Ron Wyden, which was first reported by   Motherboard and shared with Gizmodo, shows Ulysses claims to be able to “remotely geolocate” cars in “nearly any country,” with the exceptions of Cuba and North Korea. In the document, the firm explains how this might be useful to a government agency:
 The Ulysses Group provides telematics based location intelligence, in both real time and historical formats. The data can be used to geo-locate, track and target time sensitive mobile targets, tip and cue other sensors, develop patterns of life, identify networks and relationships, and enhance situational awareness among many other applications. ... Ulysses’ analysis, and existing access to bulk commercial telematics data, represents a revolutionary opportunity to collect and analyze real time data on mobile targets anywhere in the world without deploying into harms way – whether you want to geo-locate one vehicle or 25,000,000...
    It’s been   well-known for some time that as cars have become increasingly connected to the internet, they have also generated an ever-larger amount of data (this can include location, usage rates, internal media and communications preferences, external road conditions, and so on): Often, this data is being shared continuously with the automaker, with car-parts manufacturers, and sometimes with third parties. In recent years, there has been a race to sell and profit off this data, and a fairly complicated industrial ecosystem has emerged around it. A   2016 study by dreaded consulting firm McKinsey projects that, by the year 2030, the global revenue generated by car data may total anywhere from $450 billion to $750 billion.
  At the same time, federal agencies have been   enthusiastically hoovering up personal consumer data collected by private contractors like Ulysses, in an effort to augment their own surveillance and espionage operations. The Department of Homeland Security, the FBI, and countless other agencies have all been caught indulging in this trend. In the case of car location data, the spying capabilities it claims to provide are enormous, as Ulysses freely admits.
  “Vehicle location data is transmitted on a constant and near real time basis while the vehicle is operating,” Ulysses states in the document. “We believe that this one attribute will dramatically enhance military intelligence and operational capabilities, as well as reduce the costs and risk footprint of ISR assets [ intelligence, surveillance, target acquisition and reconnaissance] currently used to search for and acquire mobile targets of interest.” The company also notes that this data cache is only projected to grow: “By 2025 it is estimated that 100% of new cars will be connected [to the internet] at some level – each transmitted more than 25 gigabytes of data per hour.”
 Ulysses, based in South Carolina, says it’s staffed by former military and intelligence officials and has been active since the early 2000s , according to   the company’s website. Its president, Andrew Lewis,   previously served in a number of high-level positions within the Department of Defense and other military positions. Online records show the firm contracted with the U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM) between 2016 and 2017. The company also claims to have worked with a variety of other agencies, providing services related to drones, aerospace, and financial data. A version of the company’s website, captured by the Wayback Machine, from July 2019 reads:
 Ulysses has won multiple DOD awards to conduct financial targeting and exploitation for the U.S. Navy, U.S. Army, and MARFORCYBER, including a 2016 - 2017 award by USSOCOM to serve as the only company that supported the command within the economic and financial warfare domain. Additionally, Ulysses has supported four different GEOINT focused contracts for NGA. Ulysses has a robust UAS vertical encompassing both fixed and rotary wing platforms that are designed and/or modified for operational use inhouse leveraging our rapid prototyping capabilities.
  Maybe the worst thing about this whole story is that it’s not entirely clear where a company like Ulysses gets all its data from. Andrea Amico, the founder of Privacy4Cars   told Vice that, due to the convoluted nature of vehicle data collection, there are a whole variety of sources where locations might be procured from: “the company that provides the map itself, for instance, would have access to it; the company that provides the infotainment system may have access to it; the company that provides the traffic data may have access to it; the company that provides the parking data may have access to it. Right there and then you’ve got five companies that are getting your location.”
  A call to the Ulysses Group wasn’t immediately returned. A call to the Alliance of Automobile Manufacturers for commentary on this story didn’t garner a response either (the group   represents the interests of car giants like Ford, Honda, Subaru, Hyundai, BMW, Chrysler and others, and has previously published a set of consumer   data privacy guidelines for the industry). We will update this story if we hear back.
title: 监控公司声称它可以实时跟踪任何汽车
title_en: Surveillance Company Claims It Can Track Nearly Any Car in Real-Time

note:
十年前，大多数美国人从未对变性问题进行了谈话。现在有一个问题要求只有一个可接受的答案。 “跨性别平等是我们时代的民权问题，”拜登总统于2020年1月推断。“涉及基本人权时，没有妥协的余地。”
    例如，我们可能想谈谈竞技时最佳的政策是什么。高中女生应该对被识别为女孩的男孩失去锦标赛比赛吗？女性空间怎么样，如家庭暴力受害者的庇护所？谁应该被迫过夜，与妇女身份肯定会过夜吗？
  以及在过去十年中寻求性别重新分配程序的女孩数量造成激增？我们可以在急于得出结论之前发现这一点，封闭青春期障碍药物和交叉性激素疗法 - 甚至是13岁的双重乳房切除术 - 是人权吗？
  我们应该从英国课程。 2018年9月，U.K.妇女和均等部长向为什么女孩越来越感到不舒服，妇女和平等部长发起了调查。 2020年12月，A U.K.法院对医生“过渡”的能力严格限制了一个次要的一名未成年人，因为成年人造成了无法弥补的成年人，所以曾为过起诉国家卫生服务。
note_en:
A decade ago, most Americans had never had a conversation about transgender issues. Now a question few had asked has only one acceptable answer. “Transgender equality is the civil rights issue of our time,” President Biden tweeted in January 2020. “There is no room for compromise when it comes to basic human rights.”
    We might want to talk about what policies are best when it comes to athletics, for example. Should high-school girls be losing championship races to boys who identify as girls? How about female-only spaces, like shelters for victims of domestic violence? Should women in dire straits be forced to spend the night with men who identify as women?
  And what’s causing the surge in the number of girls seeking sex-reassignment procedures in the past decade? Might we want to find that out before we rush to conclude that puberty-blocking drugs and cross-sex hormone therapies—and even  double mastectomies for 13-year-olds—are a human right?
  We should take a lesson from the United Kingdom. In September 2018, the U.K.’s minister for women and equalities launched an investigation into why girls increasingly feel uncomfortable with their bodies. In December 2020 a U.K. court put strict restrictions on the ability of doctors to “transition” a minor—after one minor who had done so sued the National Health Service because of the irreparable damage adults had inflicted upon her body.
title: 亚马逊不会让你读书
title_en: Amazon Won’t Let You Read My Book

note:
世界卫生组织正在敦促各国继续使用AstraZeneca的Covid-19疫苗，因为将镜头与血凝块联系起来，毫无根据的恐惧继续繁忙。
 最近十多个国家最近暂停或削减了疫苗，包括德国，法国，意大利和西班牙。瑞典和卢森堡在周二暂停了疫苗接种之一。
 通过少数报道提示暂停，即人们在收到疫苗后的某个时间开发血液凝块。稍后死亡的人。
 仍然没有证据表明疫苗导致那些血栓，这在一般人群中相对普遍。然而，许多国家暂时将疫苗接种作为预防措施。
 在星期一的新闻发布会上，世卫组织首席科学家Soumya Swaminathan指出，“人们每天都会死，所以问题真的是与疫苗的联系。”
 “这就是为什么我们需要看所有数据，”她说。 “专家正在研究数据，到目前为止，我们在这些事件和疫苗之间找不到关联，因为这些事件发生在疫苗基团中的速率实际上是比你所期望的那么少一般人口同时。“
    Astrazeneca在早期的新闻稿中呼应了这一点，并指出，从欧洲和英国接种疫苗的1700万人的数据发现没有增加血液凝固事件的发病率。 
在欧洲药物署主管欧洲药物委托的联赛中，欧洲药品局负责人也播出了持续使用疫苗的强大支持。
 “我们仍然坚信阿拉西哥疫苗在预防科夫迪-19的效果，其相关的住院和死亡风险超过了副作用的风险，”克服克服说。
 尽管如此，政府仍然击中了同行的后跟的暂停按钮，可能出现出看起来骑士的政治压力和/或恐惧。
 科学家们担心疫苗接种运动中的甚至短暂的中断可能会阻碍对病毒的进步，特别是欧洲面临着第三次感染浪潮。
 在星期三的声明中，谁试图控制螺旋形势，重新对疫苗的支持。
 此时，谁认为AstraZeneca疫苗的益处超过了其风险，并建议疫苗接种持续存在。
 Astazeneca的疫苗被授权由欧洲联盟以及其他国家的WHO和监管机构使用。但是，它尚未在美国授权。该公司刚刚在美国包装了32,000名参与者试验，目前正在审查数据。该公司的发言人在声明中表示，“我们预计我们预计我们的美国第三阶段审判的数据即将推出，并且我们计划此后不久的申请紧急使用授权。”来自该试验的进一步安全数据可能会对目前对疫苗的担忧作出。
note_en:
The World Health Organization is urging countries to continue using AstraZeneca’s COVID-19 vaccine as unfounded fears linking the shots to blood clots continue to spread wildly.
 More than a dozen countries have recently suspended or curtailed use of the vaccine, including Germany, France, Italy, and Spain. Sweden and Luxembourg were among the latest to pause vaccinations on Tuesday.
 The suspensions were prompted by a handful of reports that people developed blood clots sometime after receiving the vaccine. A few of the people later died.
 There remains no evidence that the vaccine caused those blood clots, which are relatively common in the general population. However, many countries temporarily suspended vaccinations as a precautionary measure.
 In  a press briefing Monday, WHO Chief Scientist Soumya Swaminathan noted that “people die every day so the question really is the linkage with the vaccine.”
 “This is why we need to look at all of the data,” she said. “The experts are looking at the data and, so far, we do not find an association between these events and the vaccine because the rates at which these events have occurred in the vaccinated group are, in fact, less than you would expect in the general population at the same time.”
    AstraZeneca had echoed this point in  an earlier press release, noting that data from 17 million people vaccinated across Europe and the United Kingdom found no increased incidence of blood clotting events.
  In comments to the Associated Press Tuesday, the head of the European Medicines Agency, Emer Cooke, also  aired strong support for the continued use of the vaccine.
 “We are still firmly convinced that the benefits of the AstraZeneca vaccine in preventing COVID-19 with its associated risk of hospitalization and death outweigh the risk of the side effects,” Cooke said.
 Still, governments hit the pause button on the heels of their peers,  potentially out of political pressure and/or fear of appearing cavalier.
 Scientists fear that even short interruptions in vaccination campaigns may hamper progress against the virus, particularly as Europe is facing a third wave of infections.
 In a statement Wednesday, the WHO tried to gain control of the spiraling situation, restating its support for the vaccine.
 At this time, WHO considers that the benefits of the AstraZeneca vaccine outweigh its risks and recommends that vaccinations continue.
 AstraZeneca’s vaccine is authorized for use  by the WHO and regulators in European Union as well as other countries. However, it is not yet authorized in the US. The company is just now wrapping up a 32,000-participant trial in the US and the data is currently being reviewed. A spokesperson for the company said in  a statement to Reuters that “we expect data from our US Phase III trial to be available soon, and we plan to file for emergency use authorization shortly thereafter.” Further safety data from that trial could allay current fears about the vaccine.
title: 在Astrazeneca疫苗的恐慌中，敦促各国继续使用它
title_en: Amid panic over AstraZeneca vaccine, WHO urges countries to keep using it
